messages.content:
Can you please write an excel VBA event that will do the following;

Copy range B1:C21 in worksheet 'Today'
and paste values only into worksheet 'Notes' range A:B
starting from the first empty row

Sure! Here's an example of an Excel VBA event that will perform the requested action:

```vb
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Check if the selection has changed in "Today" worksheet
    If Target.Address = Range("B1:C21").Address And ActiveSheet.Name = "Today" Then
        ' Define the source range
        Dim sourceRange As Range
        Set sourceRange = Sheets("Today").Range("B1:C21")
        
        ' Define the destination range in "Notes" worksheet
        Dim destinationSheet As Worksheet
        Dim destinationRange As Range
        Set destinationSheet = Sheets("Notes")
        Set destinationRange = destinationSheet.Cells(destinationSheet.Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Offset(1, 0)
        
        ' Copy values only from source range to destination range
        sourceRange.Copy
        destinationRange.PasteSpecial xlPasteValues
        Application.CutCopyMode = False
        
        ' Clear the selection in "Today" worksheet
        Target.ClearContents
    End If
End Sub
```

To implement this macro, follow these steps:

1. Press `ALT + F11` to open the VBA Editor.
2. In the Project Explorer window, find and double-click the worksheet where the event should occur. For example, if "Today" worksheet contains the data, double-click that sheet.
3. In the code window, paste the provided code.
4. Modify the worksheet names or range addresses if necessary.
5. Close the VBA Editor.

Now, whenever you change the selection in the specified range ("B1:C21") on the "Today" worksheet, the VBA code will automatically copy the values and paste them into the first empty row in columns A and B of the "Notes" worksheet. The selection in the "Today" worksheet will then be cleared.

Please write a VBA code that does the following;

For all cells in column B that contain the value 'x'
Delete the row A:B and move the cells beneath up.
Continue the loop until both cells in the same row of column A and column B are blank.

When the  code runs, I do not want it to affect the rows in in any other column.

Certainly! Here's a VBA code that meets your requirements:

```vb
Sub DeleteRowsWithXValue()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet1") ' Replace "Sheet1" with the name of your worksheet
    
    Dim lastRow As Long
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row
    
    Dim i As Long
    i = 1
    
    Do While i <= lastRow
        If ws.Range("B" & i).Value = "x" Then
            ws.Range("A" & i & ":B" & i).Delete Shift:=xlUp
            lastRow = lastRow - 1
        Else
            i = i + 1
        End If
    Loop
End Sub
```

To use this code, follow these steps:

1. Press `ALT + F11` to open the VBA Editor.
2. In the Project Explorer window, find and double-click the module where you want to add the code, or right-click on the VBAProject and choose "Insert" -> "Module" to create a new module.
3. In the code window, paste the provided code.
4. Replace "Sheet1" with the name of your worksheet if it's different.
5. Save your workbook.
6. Press `ALT + Q` to close the VBA Editor.

When you run this macro, it will iterate through each cell in column B of the specified worksheet. If a cell contains the value "x", it will delete the entire row (columns A and B) and shift the cells beneath up. The loop will continue until both cells in the same row of column A and column B are blank. The process will only affect columns A and B and will not modify any other columns in the worksheet.
I would like a vba code that does the following;  If in the next three days a new month will start the pop up a message "Pease Back Up archived Tasks NOW!"

Certainly! Here's a VBA code that checks if a new month will start within the next three days and displays a message box if true:

```vb
Sub CheckNewMonth()
    Dim currentDate As Date
    Dim nextMonthDate As Date
    
    ' Set the current date
    currentDate = Date
    
    ' Calculate the date for the next month
    nextMonthDate = DateSerial(Year(currentDate), Month(currentDate) + 1, 1)
    
    ' Check if a new month will start within the next three days
    If nextMonthDate - currentDate <= 3 Then
        MsgBox "Please Back Up archived Tasks NOW!", vbInformation, "Important Message"
    End If
End Sub
```

To use this code, follow these steps:

1. Press `ALT + F11` to open the VBA Editor.
2. In the Project Explorer window, find and double-click the module where you want to add the code, or right-click on the VBAProject and choose "Insert" -> "Module" to create a new module.
3. In the code window, paste the provided code.
4. Save your workbook.
5. Press `ALT + Q` to close the VBA Editor.

You can then run this macro manually whenever you want to check if a new month will start within the next three days. If true, it will display a message box with the reminder "Please Back Up archived Tasks NOW!".

I have a workbook that when opened, activates sheet 'Notes'
In sheet 'Notes', I have this event; 
Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
Call CheckNewMonth
End Sub
Unfortunately, the event does not run unless I leave the sheet and come back to the sheet.
How can I get the event to run immediately when the sheet 'Notes' is activated by the workbook.

To ensure that the event runs immediately when the sheet "Notes" is activated upon opening the workbook, you can use the Workbook_Open event. This event is triggered when the workbook is opened. Here's an updated code snippet:

In ThisWorkbook module:
```vb
Private Sub Workbook_Open()
    Sheets("Notes").Activate
    Call CheckNewMonth
End Sub
```

In the "Notes" worksheet module:
```vb
Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    Call CheckNewMonth
End Sub
```

By placing the code in the Workbook_Open event, the workbook will activate the "Notes" sheet and call the CheckNewMonth macro as soon as it's opened. This will ensure that the event runs immediately without requiring the user to navigate away from the sheet and return to it.

In the code below, the sheet Today is copied to a new sheet 'newSheet.Name = "Task" & Format(Now(), "DDMMYY")'
Is there a way of copying only the range A1:F22 from sheet 'Today' to the new sheet.

Sub StartC()
    Dim wbk1 As Workbook
    Dim ws1 As Worksheet
    Dim newSheet As Worksheet
    Set wbk1 = ThisWorkbook
    Set ws1 = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Today")
    Application.ScreenUpdating = False
    ws1.Activate
    With ws1.Range("G22")
        If Not IsDate(.Value) Then
            MsgBox "No date"
        ElseIf .Value <> Date Then
            ' Create new sheet without copying VBA code
            ws1.Range("G:22").Value = ws1.Range("A:22").Value
            Set newSheet = wbk1.Sheets.Add(After:=wbk1.Sheets(wbk1.Sheets.Count))
            newSheet.Name = "Task" & Format(Now(), "DDMMYY")
            newSheet.Visible = False
            ws1.Cells.Copy Destination:=newSheet.Cells
            Application.Wait (Now + TimeValue("0:00:01"))
            ws1.Visible = True
            newSheet.Visible = True
            Debug.Print "Used range in new sheet: " & newSheet.UsedRange.Address
            Call ClearC
        Else
            MsgBox "Date Is Today"
        End If
    End With
    Application.ScreenUpdating = True
    Application.Wait (Now + TimeValue("0:00:01"))
    End
End Sub

Yes, you can modify the code to copy only the range A1:F22 from the "Today" sheet to the new sheet. Here's an updated version of the code snippet:

```vb
Sub StartC()
    Dim wbk1 As Workbook
    Dim ws1 As Worksheet
    Dim newSheet As Worksheet
    Set wbk1 = ThisWorkbook
    Set ws1 = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Today")
    Application.ScreenUpdating = False
    ws1.Activate
    With ws1.Range("G22")
        If Not IsDate(.Value) Then
            MsgBox "No date"
        ElseIf .Value <> Date Then
            ' Create new sheet without copying VBA code
            ws1.Range("A1:F22").Copy
            Set newSheet = wbk1.Sheets.Add(After:=wbk1.Sheets(wbk1.Sheets.Count))
            newSheet.Name = "Task" & Format(Now(), "DDMMYY")
            newSheet.Visible = False
            newSheet.Range("A1").PasteSpecial xlPasteValues
            Application.CutCopyMode = False
            Application.Wait (Now + TimeValue("0:00:01"))
            ws1.Visible = True
            newSheet.Visible = True
            Debug.Print "Used range in new sheet: " & newSheet.UsedRange.Address
            Call ClearC
        Else
            MsgBox "Date Is Today"
        End If
    End With
    Application.ScreenUpdating = True
    Application.Wait (Now + TimeValue("0:00:01"))
    End
End Sub
```

In this code, I replaced the line `ws1.Cells.Copy Destination:=newSheet.Cells` with the following lines:

```vb
ws1.Range("A1:F22").Copy
newSheet.Range("A1").PasteSpecial xlPasteValues
Application.CutCopyMode = False
```

This will copy only the range A1:F22 from the "Today" worksheet and paste the values in the corresponding range on the new sheet. The `Application.CutCopyMode = False` line is used to clear the clipboard after copying.

Please note that the new sheet will contain values only, without any formatting or formulas.

I have this formula in D5 '=SUMPRODUCT(LEN('2023'!I1:I500)-LEN(SUBSTITUTE('2023'!I1:I500,"x","")))'
In column J5 the value is 'I'
How can I write the formula so that all the 'I' in the formula is taken from the cell J5

To refer to the value in cell J5 within your formula in cell D5, you can concatenate it with the rest of the formula as a string. Here's the modified formula:

```vb
=SUMPRODUCT(LEN('2023'!I1:I500)-LEN(SUBSTITUTE('2023'!I1:I500, J5, "")))
```

In this formula, the value in cell J5 is referred to by using `J5` within the formula string. This way, the formula will dynamically use the value in cell J5 as the parameter for comparison throughout the formula.

Make sure to replace any existing formula in cell D5 with this modified formula.
You gave me the original formula and have provided the new formula with the concateneated approach

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to dynamically refer to the value in cell J5 within your formula in cell D5, you can use the INDIRECT function. Here's the modified formula:

```vb
=SUMPRODUCT(LEN('2023'!I1:I500)-LEN(SUBSTITUTE('2023'!I1:I500, INDIRECT("J5"), "")))
```

In this modified formula, the INDIRECT function is used to convert the text in cell J5 into a valid cell reference. This way, the formula will dynamically evaluate the value in cell J5 and use it as the parameter for comparison throughout the formula.

Please replace the existing formula in cell D5 with this updated formula.
To change ‘2023’!I1:I500 will this work ‘2023’!INDIRECT("J5")&1:INDIRECT("J5")&500

Apologies for any confusion. Unfortunately, using `‘2023’!INDIRECT("J5")&1:INDIRECT("J5")&500` directly within the formula won't work as intended. To achieve the desired outcome, you need to use the `INDIRECT` function more explicitly. Here's the modified formula:

```vb
=SUMPRODUCT(LEN(INDIRECT("2023!"&J5&"1:"&J5&"500"))-LEN(SUBSTITUTE(INDIRECT("2023!"&J5&"1:"&J5&"500"),"x","")))
```

In this modified formula, the `INDIRECT` function is used to construct the desired range reference by concatenating the text in cell J5. It will refer to the range '2023'!I1:I500 or any range determined by the value in cell J5.

Please replace the existing formula in cell D5 with this updated formula, and it will dynamically refer to the range indicated by the value in cell J5.
How can I write a formula in a cell to give me the total sum of all the cells in the column with the value 'x'

To calculate the sum of all cells in a column where the value is 'x', you can use the SUMIF function in Excel. Here's the formula you can use:

```vb
=SUMIF(A:A, "x", B:B)
```

In this formula, replace `A:A` with the column range where you have the 'x' values, and replace `B:B` with the respective column range containing the values to be summed.

For example, if you want to sum the values in column C where the corresponding cells in column A have the value 'x', you would use the following formula:

```vb
=SUMIF(A:A, "x", C:C)
```

Ensure that the range provided covers the entire column, or adjust it accordingly based on your specific data range.
My apologies, I want to count the number of cells in a column with the value 'x'

No problem! To count the number of cells in a column with the value 'x', you can use the COUNTIF function in Excel. Here's the formula you can use:

```vb
=COUNTIF(A:A, "x")
```

In this formula, replace `A:A` with the column range where you want to count the 'x' values.

For example, if you want to count the occurrences of 'x' in column B, you would use the following formula in a cell:

```vb
=COUNTIF(B:B, "x")
```

Make sure that the range provided covers the entire column or adjust it according to your specific data range. This formula will give you the count of cells in the specified column that have the value 'x'.

The Value in I2 = 2022
The value in J2 = C

How can I write this formula to use concantenate pointing at I2 and J2
='2022'!C1

I have given it a try below but it definately is not right

=INDIRECT("&I2&"&"!"&"&J2&1)

To construct a cell reference using concatenation based on the values in cells I2 and J2, you can modify the formula as follows:

```
=INDIRECT("'"&I2&"'!"&J2&1)
```

In this formula, the INDIRECT function is utilized to create the cell reference by concatenating the values in cells I2 and J2. The single quotation marks around the sheet name (I2) are necessary when the sheet name is alphanumeric or contains spaces.

For instance, when the value in I2 is "2022" and the value in J2 is "C", the resulting cell reference will be `'2022'!C1`.

Ensure that the sheet name in I2 is accurate and that the referenced cell exists; otherwise, the formula may return a #REF! error.

In my excel sheet, I have values in D2 (204), E2 (14), and F2 (21).
The value in H2 (239) is the sum of D2, E2, F2.
How will I write a formula in A2 to show the percentage of D2 in comparison to H2,

To calculate the percentage of the value in D2 relative to the sum in H2, you can use the following formula in cell A2:

```
=D2/H2*100
```

This formula divides the value in D2 by the value in H2, multiplies the result by 100, and displays the percentage.

For example, if D2 contains 204 and H2 contains 239, the formula in A2 would be:

```
=204/239*100
```

And the result displayed in A2 would be approximately 85.356%.

Ensure that the values in D2 and H2 are numeric and that you format cell A2 as a percentage to display the result correctly.
